title: Hieracium opsianthum
taxonomy: Hieracium opsianthum is a species within the plant genus Hieracium. It belongs to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification places it among the composites, which are characterized by the presence of both ray and disc florets. The genus Hieracium is itself part of the tribe Senecioneae, known for its diverse and often highly variable species. Further, the species is classified as a member of the kingdom Plantae.

parts: Unfortunately, I couldn't find any specific information about the medicinal parts of Hieracium opsianthum.
preparations:
Hieracium opsianthum preparations, often found in traditional herbal medicine, are typically made from the plant's aerial parts. However, specific information on these preparations is limited in available sources. 

The plant is known to be rich in flavonoids, saponins, and other bioactive compounds. 

Hieracium opsianthum is sometimes used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including inflammation and digestive issues. 

More research is needed to fully understand the efficacy and safety of Hieracium opsianthum preparations.
intro: Hieracium opsianthum, also known as Opsianthus hawkweed, is a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it a possible treatment for various conditions such as arthritis and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium opsianthum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Hieracium opsianthum include infusions, tinctures, and topical creams, which are used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and inflammatory disorders.
